items.label: About ZYNQ.peer
items.content:
# ZYNQ.peer

`ZYNQ.peer` is a lightweight, event-driven WebRTC wrapper built on top of PeerJS. It enables real-time peer-to-peer communication including messaging, video/audio streaming, and session management with minimal setup.

It is designed for simplicity while still offering powerful control over connections, media streams, and lifecycle handling.

---

### Core Capabilities

* Peer-to-peer messaging
* WebRTC audio/video streaming
* Session management system
* Event-driven architecture
* Auto heartbeat & disconnect detection
* Zero-config startup

---

### Features Overview

* Automatic PeerJS loading (CDN fallback)
* Real-time bitrate monitoring
* Smart reconnect / timeout handling
* Stream lifecycle management
* Broadcast and direct messaging
* Fully async API

---

## Installation

### Option 1 — ES Module (Recommended)
```html
<script type="module">
import 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@0.2.1/index.min.js'

await ZYNQ.load("peer")
const p = ZYNQ.peer

await p.start()
</script>
```

---

### Option 2 — Classic Script
```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@0.2.1/index.min.js"></script>
<script>
(async () => {
  await ZYNQ.load("peer")
  const p = ZYNQ.peer

  await p.start()
})();
</script>
```

---

### PeerJS (optional manual)
```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/peerjs@1.5.4/dist/peerjs.min.js"></script>
```

items.label: Quick Start
items.content:
# Quick Start

### 1. Import & Load
```javascript
import 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@0.2.1/index.min.js'
await ZYNQ.load("peer")
```

### 2. Start peer
```javascript
const p = ZYNQ.peer
await p.start()
```

### 3. Listen for readiness
```javascript
p.on('ready', id => {
  console.log('My ID:', id)
})
```

### 4. Connect to peer
```javascript
await p.connect('peer-id')
```

### 5. Send message
```javascript
p.sendTo('peer-id', 'Hello')
```

### 6. Listen messages
```javascript
p.on('message', ({ peerId, message }) => {
  console.log(peerId, message)
})
```

items.label: Start Options
items.content:
# Start Options

### Example
```javascript
await p.start({
  host: 'your-server.com',
  port: 443,
  secure: true
})
```

These options are passed to PeerJS.

---

### Debug Level
```javascript
p.setDebug(2)
```

| Level | Meaning |
|------|--------|
| 0 | Silent |
| 1 | Basic |
| 2 | Verbose |
| 3 | Full debug |

---

### Auto Start
```javascript
p.setAutoStart(true)
```

items.label: Properties
items.content:
# Properties

| Property | Type | Description |
|---------|------|------------|
| peer | PeerJS | internal peer |
| sessions | Map | active sessions |
| localStream | MediaStream | camera stream |
| ready | boolean | ready state |
| myId | string | peer id |

---

```javascript
p.getMyId()
p.getActivePeers()
```

items.label: Methods
items.content:
# Methods

### Core
- start(options)
- connect(peerId, config)
- disconnect(peerId)
- destroy()

---

### Messaging
- sendTo(peerId, msg)
- broadcast(msg)

---

### Utils
- getMyId()
- getActivePeers()
- setDebug(level)
- setAutoStart(bool)

items.label: Event System
items.content:
# Events

### Usage
```javascript
p.on('event', cb)
p.off('event', cb)
```

---

### Events List

| Event | Description |
|------|------------|
| ready | peer ready |
| invite | incoming call |
| joined | connected |
| left | disconnected |
| message | message received |
| remote-stream | video/audio remote |
| local-stream | local camera |
| bitrate | network stats |
| error | error |
| destroyed | destroyed |

---

### Example
```javascript
p.on('invite', ({ accept }) => accept())
```

items.label: Media Config
items.content:
# Media

### Connect with media
```javascript
p.connect('id', {
  video: true,
  audio: true,
  resolution: 720,
  fps: 30
})
```

---

### Stream event
```javascript
p.on('remote-stream', ({ stream }) => {
  video.srcObject = stream
})
```

items.label: Sessions
items.content:
# Sessions

Each peer is stored as a session.

### Structure
```javascript
{
  conn,
  call,
  config,
  lastSeen,
  status
}
```

---

### Flow
1. REQ
2. ACC / REJ
3. Media setup
4. Heartbeat
5. Disconnect

---

### Timeout
~12s inactivity disconnect

items.label: Heartbeat
items.content:
# Heartbeat

Runs every 2 seconds:

- PING/PONG
- bitrate tracking
- timeout detection

---

```javascript
p.on('bitrate', ({ peerId, kbps }) => {
  console.log(kbps)
})
```

items.label: Destroy
items.content:
# Destroy

```javascript
p.destroy()
```

Cleans:
- sessions
- streams
- events
- peer instance
